name: Pentagon Attack Questions
description: The Pentagon Attack Questions refer to a cluster of conspiracy theories surrounding the events of September 11, 2001, specifically concerning the attack on the Pentagon. While the official narrative states that American Airlines Flight 77 crashed into the Pentagon, killing 184 people, proponents of these theories question key aspects of this account. These questions often center on the nature of the aircraft that struck the building, the extent of the damage, and the evidence presented in the official investigation. Common claims include the assertion that a missile, drone, or smaller aircraft struck the Pentagon, rather than a Boeing 757, or that the damage was pre-existing and the attack was an inside job designed to justify the subsequent wars in Afghanistan and Iraq. These theories gained traction shortly after the attacks, fueled by skepticism towards the government and a desire to understand the seemingly inexplicable. Proponents often cite alleged inconsistencies in eyewitness accounts, the lack of readily visible wreckage of a Boeing 757, and purported anomalies in the flight path data. However, mainstream investigations, including the 9/11 Commission Report and reports from the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) and the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE), thoroughly investigated the evidence and concluded that Flight 77 did indeed strike the Pentagon. These investigations utilized recovered flight data recorders, DNA evidence, analysis of the crash site, and eyewitness testimonies to support their findings. Critics of the conspiracy theories point to the overwhelming evidence supporting the official narrative, the lack of credible evidence for alternative explanations, and the potential harm caused by spreading misinformation about a tragic event.
subtitle: Doubts Surrounding the 9/11 Pentagon Attack

claims.claim: A missile or drone, not a Boeing 757, struck the Pentagon.
claims.evidence: Lack of visible wreckage of a Boeing 757, small size of the hole in the Pentagon, eyewitness accounts describing a smaller aircraft.
claims.criticism: Recovered flight data recorders confirm Flight 77 was a Boeing 757. The hole size is consistent with the aircraft's impact. Eyewitness accounts are often unreliable and subject to misinterpretation.

claims.claim: The damage to the Pentagon was pre-existing or caused by controlled demolition.
claims.evidence: Photographs showing limited damage immediately after the attack, claims of unusual structural failures.
claims.criticism: Structural analysis demonstrates the damage was consistent with the impact of a large aircraft. Controlled demolition is not supported by any credible evidence and would have required extensive planning and coordination.

claims.claim: The official flight path data for Flight 77 is inaccurate or fabricated.
claims.evidence: Discrepancies between reported flight path and eyewitness accounts, claims of manipulated radar data.
claims.criticism: Flight path data is based on multiple sources, including radar data and flight data recorders, and is considered reliable. Minor discrepancies are expected and do not invalidate the overall findings.

keyFigures.name: Thierry Meyssan
keyFigures.role: Author of '9/11: The Big Lie,' a prominent book promoting the missile theory.

keyFigures.name: David Ray Griffin
keyFigures.role: Theologian and author who questioned the official account of 9/11, including the Pentagon attack.

keyFigures.name: David Dunbar
keyFigures.role: Co-author of 'Debunking 9/11 Myths,' which refutes many of the conspiracy theories.

keyFigures.name: Jim Dwyer
keyFigures.role: New York Times journalist who covered the 9/11 attacks and debunked many conspiracy theories.

mythologyConnections: The idea of a hidden hand manipulating events
The distrust of authority figures and official narratives
The search for truth and meaning in the face of tragedy

timeline.event: American Airlines Flight 77 crashes into the Pentagon.
Conspiracy theories questioning the official account begin to emerge.
The 9/11 Commission Report is released, concluding that Flight 77 struck the Pentagon.
Debate and discussion surrounding the Pentagon attack and 9/11 conspiracy theories continue.

sources.author: David Ray Griffin
sources.work: The New Pearl Harbor: Disturbing Questions About the Bush Administration and 9/11
sources.title: David Ray Griffin

sources.author: David Dunbar and Brad Reagan
sources.work: Debunking 9/11 Myths: Why Conspiracy Theories Can't Stand Up to the Facts
sources.title: David Dunbar and Brad Reagan
